title: Operating Systems & Desktop
subtitle: Identify Desktop, Taskbar & Start Menu
overview: This session introduces students to the basic parts of a computer operating system including the desktop, taskbar, icons, and start menu using real-life examples and activities.

images._note: Add classroom photos, desktop screenshots, and student activity images.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Taskbar image from classroom computer
gallery.caption: Taskbar helps open and switch programs
Start Menu shows apps and settings
Icons are shortcuts to programs and files

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students using computers in lab
gallery.caption: Students identifying desktop parts

keyTopics: Operating System — The brain that controls the computer
Desktop — Main screen of the computer
Taskbar — Bar used to open and switch programs
Start Menu — Place to access apps and settings
Recycle Bin — Stores deleted files temporarily
Mouse Click — Used to open files and programs

youtubeVideos.title: Introduction to Operating System
youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of operating systems for beginners.

youtubeVideos.title: Desktop, Taskbar and Start Menu Explained
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn the parts of the Windows desktop.

youtubeVideos.title: Computer Basics for Kids
youtubeVideos.desc: Introduction to computer basics in an easy way.

activity.title: Identify Desktop Parts Activity
steps: Wait for desktop screen to appear
Find the taskbar at the bottom
Open Paint or Calculator from Start Menu
Close the application using X button

options: A software that controls the computer
quiz.explanation: An Operating System controls all computer activities and helps users interact with the computer.

options: The main screen of the computer
quiz.explanation: Desktop is the main screen that appears after the computer starts.

quiz.q: Where is the Taskbar usually located?
quiz.explanation: The taskbar is usually located at the bottom of the screen.

quiz.q: What does the Start Menu help us do?
quiz.explanation: The Start Menu helps users access apps, files, and settings.
Icons are small images used to open programs or files quickly.

quiz.q: Which device is mainly used to click icons?
quiz.explanation: The mouse is used to click and open icons.

quiz.q: Which icon stores deleted files temporarily?
quiz.explanation: Recycle Bin temporarily stores deleted files.
Wallpaper is the background image shown on the desktop.

quiz.q: Which button is used to close a window?
quiz.explanation: The X button closes the current window or application.

quiz.q: Which of these is an Operating System?
quiz.explanation: Windows is a popular operating system.
